zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Hulton於大約1523年出生在英國藍卡爾法爾沃斯時，她的父親Alexander Hulton36歲，母親Jane Stones21歲。她於大約1540年與Thomas Adami Rothwell在英國藍卡爾法爾沃斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Hulton于大约1523年出生在英国藍卡爾法爾沃斯时，她的父亲Alexander Hulton36岁，母亲Jane Stones21岁。她于大约1540年与Thomas Adami Rothwell在英国藍卡爾法爾沃斯结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Hulton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Hulton (Lancashire) or Hulton (Staffordshire), both from Old English hyll ‘hill’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Hilton .

variants.name: Hilton
meanings: habitational name from any of various places called Hilton (e.g. in Cambridgeshire, Derbyshire, Staffordshire, North Yorkshire, and Westmorland), most of which derive from Old English hyll ‘hill’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some come from Old English helde ‘slope’ + tūn.

occasionally, as in the case of Ralph filius Hilton (Yorkshire 1219), perhaps from the personal name Hilton, itself a possible form of Norman Hildun, composed of the ancient Germanic elements hild ‘strife, battle’ + hūn ‘bear cub’. Compare Hilt . This English surname is present in Ireland (mainly taken to Ulster in the early 17th century, though recorded earlier in Dublin).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Hulton was born about 1523, in Farnworth, Lancashire, England, her father, Alexander Hulton, was 36 and her mother, Jane Stones, was 21. She married Thomas Adami Rothwell about 1540, in Farnworth, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Hulton est née environ 1523, à Farnworth, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Alexander Hulton, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Jane Stones, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Adami Rothwell environ 1540, à Farnworth, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Hulton um 1523 in Farnworth, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Alexander Hulton 36 und ihre Mutter Jane Stones 21. Sie hat um 1540 in Farnworth, Lancashire, England Thomas Adami Rothwell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Hulton è nata il circa 1523 nel Farnworth, Lancashire, England, sua padre, Alexander Hulton, aveva 36 anni e sua madre, Jane Stones, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Adami Rothwell circa 1540, nel Farnworth, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hultonが約1523年にイギリスランカシュアイアファルンウオルスで生まれた時，父Alexander Hultonは36歳，母Jane Stonesは21歳であった。約1540年にイギリスランカシュアイアファルンウオルスでThomas Adami Rothwellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hulton(이)가 대략 1523년 영국 랭카실르 패른워스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Alexander Hulton(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jane Stones(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 대략 1540년 영국 랭카실르 패른워스에서 Thomas Adami Rothwell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Hulton nasceu aproximadamente 1523, em Farnworth, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Alexander Hulton, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Jane Stones, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Adami Rothwell aproximadamente 1540, em Farnworth, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Hulton родилась около 1523 г. в местности фарнворт, ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени Alexander Hulton было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane Stones, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Adami Rothwell около 1540 г. в местности фарнворт, ланкашир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Hulton nació aproximadamente 1523, en Farnworth, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Alexander Hulton, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Jane Stones, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Thomas Adami Rothwell aproximadamente 1540, en Farnworth, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
